Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class TransportRequest

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.TransportRequest
public final class TransportRequest extends Object
A fully-resolved, transport-layer HTTP request.

All values (URIs, headers, bodies) are evaluated before this object is constructed — suppliers have been invoked, null-valued parts have been filtered, and the final URI has been assembled with query parameters and path substitutions applied.

Transport implementations receive a TransportRequest and are responsible for executing it against the remote server, returning a TransportResponse.

Beta — API subject to change.

Since:
9.2.1

  • Method Details

    • builder

      public static TransportRequest.Builder builder()
      Returns a new TransportRequest.Builder.

    • getMethod

      public String getMethod()
      Returns the HTTP method (e.g. "GET", "POST").
      Returns:
      The method. Never null.

    • getUri

      public URI getUri()
      Returns the fully-resolved request URI, including query string.
      Returns:
      The URI. Never null.

    • getHeaders

      public List<TransportHeader> getHeaders()
      Returns all request headers in the order they should be sent.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable list. Never null.

    • getFirstHeader

      public TransportHeader getFirstHeader(String name)
      Returns the first header with the given name (case-insensitive), or null if absent.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The first matching header, or null.

    • getBody

      public TransportBody getBody()
      Returns the request body, or null if this is a body-less request (e.g. GET, HEAD).
      Returns:
      The body, possibly null.

    • getTimeout

      public Duration getTimeout()
      Returns the per-call response timeout, or null if none was set.

      Transports apply this as the response/read timeout for the request; a null value means the transport's own default applies. Connect timeouts remain a client-level concern.

      Returns:
      The response timeout, possibly null.
